title: Line Cook

rationales.taskAutomation: Line cooks execute standardized recipes under time pressure, a physically demanding task that's automatable only at the margins (prep, portioning).
rationales.toolMaturity: Some high-volume kitchens use robotic fry stations and portioning tools, but general-purpose line-cooking robotics remain immature.
rationales.adoption: Adoption is confined to a handful of high-volume chains piloting single-station robotics; most kitchens run entirely on human line cooks.
rationales.agenticExposure: Prep-list generation and ingredient reordering are agent-ownable; executing the line during service is not.

tasksAtRisk: Ingredient prep and portioning to spec
tasksGrowing: Multitasking across stations during a rush
Quality control and plate consistency under pressure
Adapting to last-minute menu or ticket changes
salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 35-2014, p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: Multi-station speed and consistency under service pressure
Kitchen inventory and prep-planning tool fluency
Cross-training toward sous chef or kitchen management

insight: Single-station robotics (fry baskets, portioning arms) have entered a handful of high-volume kitchens, but running a full line during a dinner rush is still beyond what any deployed system handles end to end.
